name: I slipped and fell at work in Murray yesterday, what do I do first?

mainEntity.name: I slipped and fell at work in Murray yesterday, what do I do first?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report your work injury to your employer in writing immediately (within 180 days in Utah) and seek medical care.[5]
acceptedAnswer.description:
**Report the injury to your employer immediately, ideally in writing within 180 days under Utah law.**

On-the-job accidents in Utah are typically covered by workers' compensation, not personal injury lawsuits against your employer.[5] This no-fault system provides medical benefits and wage replacement without proving fault.

- Notify your supervisor or HR verbally and follow up with a written incident report.
- Seek medical attention right away and inform doctors it's a work injury.
- Keep records of all treatments, bills, and lost wages.

File a formal claim through your employer's workers' comp insurance if not initiated automatically. Consult a workers' comp attorney if denied or facing delays.[5]

Utah's strict timelines make prompt action essential to protect your benefits.

description: Report your work injury to your employer in writing immediately (within 180 days in Utah) and seek medical care.[5]
